audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra clean-unclean animal text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for land animals, water creatures, birds, swarming things, carcass impurity, vessels, and holiness refrain. Strong anti-disgust/body-shame safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying to them,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: These are the living creatures that you may eat from all beasts that are upon earth.
Whatever divides hoof, splits cleft in hoof, and brings up cud among beasts, that you may eat.
But these you shall not eat from those bringing up cud or dividing hoof: camel, for it brings up cud but hoof it does not divide; unclean it is to you.
And hyrax, for it brings up cud but hoof it does not divide; unclean it is to you.
And hare, for it brings up cud but hoof it does not divide; unclean it is to you.
And pig, for it divides hoof and splits cleft in hoof, but cud it does not chew; unclean it is to you.
From their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcass you shall not touch; unclean they are to you.
These you may eat from all that are in waters: all that has fin and scale in waters, in seas and streams, them you may eat.
But all that has no fin and scale in seas and streams, from every swarming thing of waters and every living soul/life in waters, detestable/ritually prohibited they are to you.
They shall be detestable/ritually prohibited to you; from their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcass you shall detest/avoid.
All that has no fin and scale in waters, detestable/ritually prohibited it is to you.
These you shall detest/avoid from birds; they shall not be eaten, detestable/ritually prohibited they are: eagle, vulture, black vulture,
kite and falcon according to its kind,
every raven according to its kind,
ostrich, nighthawk, gull, hawk according to its kind,
little owl, cormorant, great owl,
white owl, pelican, carrion vulture,
stork, heron according to its kind, hoopoe, and bat.
All winged swarming things walking on four, detestable/ritually prohibited they are to you.
Yet these you may eat from all winged swarming things walking on four: those that have jointed legs above their feet, to leap with them upon earth.
These from them you may eat: locust according to its kind, bald locust according to its kind, cricket according to its kind, and grasshopper according to its kind.
All other winged swarming things that have four feet, detestable/ritually prohibited they are to you.
By these you shall become unclean; everyone touching their carcass shall be unclean until evening.
Everyone carrying from their carcass shall wash garments and be unclean until evening.
Every beast that divides hoof but does not split cleft or does not bring up cud is unclean to you; everyone touching them shall be unclean.
All that walks on paws among all living creatures walking on four are unclean to you; everyone touching their carcass shall be unclean until evening.
The one carrying their carcass shall wash garments and be unclean until evening; unclean they are to you.
These are unclean to you among swarming things swarming on earth: mole-rat, mouse, great lizard according to its kind,
gecko, monitor lizard, lizard, skink, and chameleon.
These are unclean to you among all swarming things; everyone touching them when dead shall be unclean until evening.

Anything upon which one of them falls when dead shall be unclean, any vessel of wood, garment, skin, sack, any vessel with which work is done; it shall be put into water and be unclean until evening, then clean.
Any earthen vessel into which one of them falls, all that is inside it shall be unclean, and it you shall break.
Any food that may be eaten, upon which water comes, shall be unclean; and any drink that may be drunk in any vessel shall be unclean.
Everything upon which any of their carcass falls shall be unclean; oven and hearth shall be broken down; unclean they are and unclean they shall be to you.
Yet spring or cistern, gathering of water, shall be clean; but one touching their carcass shall be unclean.
If any of their carcass falls on any seed for sowing that is to be sown, it is clean.
But if water is put on seed and any of their carcass falls on it, unclean it is to you.
If any beast from which you may eat dies, one touching its carcass shall be unclean until evening.
The one eating from its carcass shall wash garments and be unclean until evening; the one carrying its carcass shall wash garments and be unclean until evening.
Every swarming thing that swarms upon earth is detestable/ritually prohibited; it shall not be eaten.
Whatever goes on belly, whatever goes on four, or whatever has many feet, among all swarming things that swarm on earth, you shall not eat them, for detestable/ritually prohibited they are.
Do not make your souls/lives detestable with any swarming thing that swarms, and do not make yourselves unclean by them and become unclean through them.
For I am YHWH your Divine; sanctify yourselves and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not make your souls/lives unclean with any swarming thing that creeps on earth.
For I am YHWH who brought you up from land of Egypt to be to you as Divine; you shall be holy, for I am holy.
This is Torah/instruction of beast and bird and every living soul/life moving in waters and every soul/life swarming on earth,
to distinguish between unclean and clean, and between living creature that may be eaten and living creature that shall not be eaten.

translation_notes: Clean/unclean is ritual classification, not a statement that animals or bodies are disgusting or morally evil.
Detestable/ritually prohibited terms require careful lexical audit; avoid importing hatred or contempt.
Dietary distinctions are tied to holiness and Exodus identity in vv.44-45.
Carcass impurity is temporary and often resolved by washing and time until evening.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 11 for body-shame, disgust toward animals/people, ethnic contempt, or modern purity policing. It is ritual food/handling instruction within Israel's covenant identity.
